markdown:
![The French Revolution of 1848, also known as the February Revolution, ended the rule of King Louis-Philippe and led to the establishment of the Second French Republic. ](https://cdn.sanity.io/images/wla69xp2/production/96be7daa3e0f1d9aedeaf8472fef22e87ddce540-1000x1000.jpg)

## **1848 Revolution and the Second Republic**


1. The February 1848 Revolution began with protests against **economic hardship** (high unemployment, food shortages) and demands for **electoral reform**.
2. Demonstrations in Paris forced **Louis Philippe to abdicate**, and the **Second Republic** was proclaimed.
3. Universal **male suffrage** was introduced, giving millions of Frenchmen the vote for the first time.
4. The Republic promised social reforms, including **National Workshops** to provide jobs for the unemployed.

## **Repression and Division**


1. The new government was divided between **moderates**, who wanted order, and **radicals/socialists**, who demanded deeper social reforms.
2. The **June Days (1848)** saw violent clashes in Paris after the closure of the National Workshops.
3. This repression showed that the new Republic was **unwilling to pursue radical social change** and highlighted the deep divide between classes.

![Charles-Louis-Napoléon Bonaparte, best known as Napoleon III, was the nephew of Napoleon I. He was the first and only President of the Second French Republic before becoming emperor.](https://cdn.sanity.io/images/wla69xp2/production/8ff83789d99df8700f1ce75074d6abc4f2c36a61-1200x629.jpg)

## **Emergence of Louis-Napoleon Bonaparte**


1. In December 1848, **Louis-Napoleon Bonaparte**, nephew of Napoleon I, was elected president by an overwhelming majority.
2. His name symbolized **stability, order, and national glory**, appealing to peasants, conservatives, and workers alike.
3. Over time, Louis-Napoleon clashed with the Assembly over power.
4. In **1851**, he staged a **coup d’état**, dissolving the Assembly.

## **Establishment of the Second Empire (1852)**


1. In 1852, Louis-Napoleon declared himself **Emperor Napoleon III**, ending the Second Republic and beginning the **Second Empire**.
2. His rule promised** strong leadership, modernization, and a return of French greatness, **while restricting political freedoms.

<callout type="warning">1. Students sometimes treat the **1848 Revolution** as a success for democracy, forgetting how quickly it turned into repression and authoritarianism.
2. Some confuse **Louis-Napoleon** with **Napoleon I**, without explaining how his popularity came more from his _name and image_ than from his actual policies at first.</callout>

<callout type="exam_technique">* Structure essays around **phases** (Revolution → Second Republic → Repression → Rise of Louis-Napoleon → Second Empire) to show change over time.
* When discussing Louis-Napoleon’s rise, always link his popularity to **both social divisions and his Bonaparte legacy**.</callout>

<callout type="self_review">* Why did the February 1848 Revolution break out, and what changes did it bring at first?
* What were the causes and consequences of the **June Days**?
* Why was Louis-Napoleon so popular in the 1848 presidential election?</callout>
